objectiveEn: About one hour. Learn 20 animal/nature words, plural nouns (regular -s/-es and irregular), listening, speaking, homework.
objectiveMn: Ойролцоогоор 1 цаг. Амьтан, байгалийн 20 шинэ үг, олон тооны нэрс (-s/-es ба дүрмийн бус), сонсох, ярих, гэрийн даалгавар.
contentEn: Today you learn 20 animal and nature words and how to form regular and irregular plural nouns in English.
contentMn: Өнөөдөр амьтан, байгалийн 20 үг сурч, англи хэлний хэвийн ба дүрмийн бус олон тооны нэрсийг хэрхэн зөв үүсгэхийг сурна.

vocabulary.example: I can see many birds today.
The fish are in the river.
There are many trees in the park.
The forest is green in summer.
Brush your teeth twice a day!

quiz.question: What is the plural of 'sheep'?
quiz.explanation: Sheep stays the same in singular and plural.

quiz.question: Which group adds -es to form plural?
One city → many _____
quiz.explanation: Consonant + y → change y to ies.

quiz.question: What animals does Bat mention first?
Are there many sheep in Mongolia?
quiz.explanation: Sarnai says: 'There are millions of sheep! More than people!'

quiz.question: Choose the correct sentence:
quiz.explanation: Children is the correct irregular plural of child.

quiz.question: One bus → many _____
quiz.explanation: Words ending in -s add -es to form the plural.

page1.grammarExplanation: Most English nouns form plurals by adding -S: dog→dogs. Nouns ending in -s, -x, -ch, -sh add -ES: bus→buses, box→boxes. Nouns ending in consonant+y change y→IES: city→cities. IRREGULAR plurals must be memorized: child→children, man→men, woman→women, tooth→teeth, foot→feet, sheep→sheep, fish→fish, mouse→mice. / Ихэнх англи нэрс -S нэмж олон тоо үүсгэдэг. -s, -x, -ch, -sh-оор төгссөн нэрс -ES нэмнэ. Гийгүүлэгч+y→IES. ДҮРМИЙН БУС олон тооны үгсийг цээжлэх ёстой.
commonMistakes: The childrens are playing. → The CHILDREN are playing.
Two sheeps are here. → Two SHEEP are here.
The womans are here. → The WOMEN are here.

page2.readingPassage: Mongolia has amazing nature. There are beautiful mountains in the north and huge grasslands in the south. Many horses, cows, and sheep live on the steppe. The rivers are full of fish. In the forests, many birds and wild animals live. Mongolian children love nature. They play outside with their families and dogs. Mongolia's nature is truly unique and beautiful!
speakingQuestions: Do you have any pets? / Та гэрийн тэжээвэр амьтантай юу?
What animals do you see in Mongolia?
Have you ever seen a horse?
Are there many trees where you live?

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Do you have any pets at home?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes! I have two dogs and one cat.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: The dogs are Tsagaan and Khar. The cat is Minii.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: What animals do you see in Mongolia?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: We see horses, sheep, cows, and many birds.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Are there many horses in Mongolia?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes! There are more horses than people in Mongolia!

page2.speakingTip: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep talking! Every mistake helps you learn faster. Confidence is more important than perfection at this stage.

page3.listeningScript: Teacher: Today we talk about animals and nature. Bat, what animals do you see in Mongolia? Bat: We see horses, sheep, cows, and many dogs. There are also wolves and eagles! Teacher: How many sheep are there in Mongolia? Sarnai: There are millions of sheep! More than people! Teacher: What about children? Do Mongolian children spend time outdoors? Bat: Yes! Mongolian children love nature. We play outside with dogs and ride horses. Teacher: Beautiful! Mongolia's nature sounds amazing.

listeningQuestions.question: What animals does Bat mention first?
What wild animals does Bat mention?
Are there many sheep in Mongolia?
Who says there are millions of sheep?
What do children do outdoors?
options: play with dogs and ride horses
listeningQuestions.answer: play with dogs and ride horses

listeningQuestions.question: How does the teacher feel about Mongolia?
Which group adds -es to make plural?

homework: Writing: Write 5 sentences about animals in Mongolia using correct plural forms.
Vocabulary: Write 10 irregular plurals 3 times each — memorize them!
Speaking: Describe your favorite animal in 3-4 sentences.
Review: child→children · man→men · woman→women · tooth→teeth · sheep→sheep · fish→fish
completionSummary: Mastered regular and irregular plural nouns
Learned irregular plurals: child/children, man/men, sheep/sheep
